title: iterable_as_array swallows MemoryError and KeyboardInterrupt
author: devdanzin
body:
Part of #348 (C extension analysis). [Full report](https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e8a25ea988dc0461c2fd378efd84a8a3).

## Finding 3: `PyErr_Clear()` clears ALL exceptions from `PyObject_GetIter()`

**Line 2916.** When `iterable_as_array=True`, the code tries `PyObject_GetIter()` and falls back to the default encoder on failure. But `PyErr_Clear()` clears ALL exceptions — not just `TypeError` (the expected "not iterable" error). `MemoryError`, `KeyboardInterrupt`, and other non-TypeError exceptions from `__iter__` are silently swallowed.

**Reproducer (100% reproducible):**
```python
import simplejson

class BadIter:
    def __iter__(self):
        raise MemoryError("OOM in __iter__")

simplejson.dumps(BadIter(), iterable_as_array=True)
# Actual:   TypeError: Object of type BadIter is not JSON serializable
# Expected: MemoryError: OOM in __iter__
```

Same for `KeyboardInterrupt`:
```python
class KBIter:
    def __iter__(self):
        raise KeyboardInterrupt()

simplejson.dumps(KBIter(), iterable_as_array=True)
# Actual:   TypeError: Object of type KBIter is not JSON serializable
# Expected: KeyboardInterrupt
```

**Suggested fix:**
```c
if (newobj == NULL) {
    if (PyErr_ExceptionMatches(PyExc_TypeError))
        PyErr_Clear();
    else
        break;  // propagate non-TypeError exceptions
}
```

There's a similar pattern in `_call_json_method` (line 403) where `PyErr_Clear` on attribute lookup swallows all exceptions, not just `AttributeError`.
